sglang.title: fix data racing due to mutable reference using deepcopy
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Objects including BatchTokenIDOut and BatchEmbeddingOut hold mutable references to ongoing requests, which could cause data racing when the order of accesses are not strictly ordered.

Modifications
Using field to ensure list object is copied and deepcopy to remove all possible mutable re

sglang.title: Sampler cudagraph
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR fixes the bench_latency correctness problem.
When the sampler merged into the CUDA graph, the next_token_ids became a tensor with fixed physical address, which can always change mutably.
Modifications
tolist()

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.
 Add

vllm.title: [Core] Async_output_proc: Add virtual engine support (towards pipeline parallel)
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This a small PR that adds virtual engine support to the asynchronous post-processor, which is necessary to allow pipeline parallel.

vllm.title: [Performance] Enable chunked prefill and prefix caching together
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reference PRs: #6144, #6819
Make @sighingnow and @Juelianqvq as co-authors of this PR.
This PR supports prefix caching and chunked prefill to be enabled together. Different from the reference PRs, this PR simplifies the logic of dealing with partial blocks (thanks to @rkooo567 for the suggestion). H

vllm.title: [Doc] fix the autoAWQ example
vllm.author: stas00
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes: #7717
As explained in #7717 the current quantization example at https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/quantization/auto_awq.html can't be run.
I replaced this with another example I found at autoawq repo that actually works.
cc: @robertgshaw2-neuralmagic

vllm.title: [Bugfix][VLM] Fix incompatibility between #7902 and #7230
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes an incompatibility between multimodal tensor stacking and multi-image support in LLaVA-NeXT and InternVL which causes the VLM CI to fail.
It also updates the docstrings about the input shapes which have become outdated since #7230.
The issue failed to be caught in #7902 because the cha

vllm.title: [Core][Kernels] Use FlashInfer backend for FP8 KV Cache when available.
vllm.author: pavanimajety
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR enables FlashInfer when --kv-cache-dtype=fp8 is set.
Reason- on H200 we see 20% perf improvement compared to Xformers backend with paged_attention_v1 kernel that uses fp8 datatype.
This PR makes use of the fp8 support added by flashinfer in [v0.1.4]. (https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashin

vllm.title: [Kernel] [Triton] [AMD] Adding Triton implementations awq_dequantize and awq_gemm to support AWQ
vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR provides Triton implementation for awq_dequantize and awq_gemm along with accompanying unit tests and a few other changes to make it possible to run an AWQ model when the machine is not NV, e.g. ROCm.
This does not provide a fully optimized implementation, but further additions to improve ov

vllm.title: [Doc] fix 404 link
vllm.author: stas00
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fixes a broken link pointing to the correct url.

vllm.title: [Kernel/Model] Migrate mamba_ssm and causal_conv1d kernels to vLLM
vllm.author: mzusman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In order to accelate the developement/optimization of those kernels, I started to migrate the relevant code from mamba_ssm /casusal_conv1d kernels to vLLM.
This is due to several failed attempts to push several improvements to those repos, such as:

state-spaces/mamba#488 - Add prefill chunk support

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Make torch registration of punica ops optional
vllm.author: bnellnm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The punica ops only need to be registered as torch ops for dynamo.  If an older version of pytorch is being used then they can fall back to regular functions.
cc @youkaichao , @SageMoore , @mgoin , @gshtras , @jeejeelee


 PR Checklist (Click to Expand) 
Thank you for your contribution to vLLM! Befo

vllm.title: [torch.compile] avoid Dynamo guard evaluation overhead
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We have 2 types of runtime overhead in TPU:

Dynamo guard evaluation overhead, chooses which code to run
torch xla overhead, convert function input to xla input

We can manage to remove the first one, via adding one layer dispatcher above Dynamo.
I did systematic measurement this time, and find that

vllm.title: [Frontend] Minor optimizations to zmq decoupled front-end
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Avoid copying message bytes (can unpickle directly from the zmq frame)
Split proxy into simpler concurrent inbound and outbound tasks
Use pickle instead of cloudpickle for response messages
Use tuples instead of lists (cpython reuses these)

Still testing but it looks like this might actually give ~

vllm.title: [torch.compile] remove reset
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
since we skip the dynamo runtime overhead in #7898 , we don't need to reset the code after profiling anymore.
as a trade-off, we need more tests for the compilation result when dynamo is turned on, so that we can have confidence that the custom dispatcher works as expected.

vllm.title: [VLM][Core] Fix exceptions on ragged NestedTensors
vllm.author: petersalas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Two followups from #7902 related to NestedTensor handling:

#7929 used is_list_of which subtly changed a condition from "any item is a list" to "the first item is a list" -- this restores the behavior and adds a test that covers it.
When flattening NestedTensors we need to actually flatten recursive
